content.problemStatement: Write a program that first accepts the number of test cases **t**. For each test case, read an integer **num**. Check if **num** is even using the **isEven** function. If **num** is even, output "Even"; otherwise, output "Odd".
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer t, the number of test cases. Each of the next t lines contains a single integer num.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing "Even" if num is even, otherwise output "Odd".

sampleTestCases.explanation: 2 and 6 are divisible by 2, so they are Even. 5 leaves remainder 1, so it is Odd.
0 and -2 are multiples of 2 → Even. -1 and 7 are not multiples of 2 → Odd.

solution.approach: Read the integer t. For each of the t test cases read num as a 64‑bit signed integer. An integer is even iff num % 2 == 0 (the remainder when divided by 2 is zero). Print "Even" for even numbers and "Odd" for odd numbers. The algorithm is O(t) time and O(1) extra space (apart from the output buffer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(t) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of t.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int t; if(scanf("%d",&t)!=1) return 0; while(t--){ long long num; scanf("%lld",&num); if(num%%2==0) printf("Even\n"); else printf("Odd\n"); } return 0; }
